collection_method: LLM-simulated user interview based on scenario prompts
scenario: A user opens the 3DGS Viewer dashboard, reviews DORA/release controls, checks model status, and decides whether the viewer is useful for a small-product scan workflow.

participants.pattern: Explores metrics first, then checks docs and APIs.
participants.feedback: The dashboard proves the DevOps pipeline quickly, but I wanted a clearer link from the metrics to the actual 3DGS viewer workflow.

participants.pattern: Audits reproducibility, CI evidence, and rollout logs.
participants.feedback: The compact release-control panel makes it easier to verify feature flags and experiments without reading the source first.

participants.pattern: Looks for PASS/FAIL status and operational clarity.
participants.feedback: The charts are readable, but I need a more obvious inspection result table and less engineering terminology.

participants.pattern: Checks page load, responsive layout, and visual hierarchy.
participants.feedback: The release controls are useful, and the compact variant reduces scrolling. Mobile layout should be tested next.

participants.pattern: Calls health, flags, and event APIs before using the UI.
participants.feedback: The /flags and /events endpoints make the experiment measurable. I would add schema validation before production.

participants.pattern: Looks for KPI deltas and go/no-go decisions.
participants.feedback: The KPI framing is strong, but the dashboard should show the current experiment hypothesis and decision status.

participants.pattern: Reviews README, workflows, tests, and contribution readiness.
participants.feedback: The repo is easy to review because workflows, docs, and experiment logs are linked. Keep generated reports small.

participants.pattern: Checks whether metrics connect to 3DGS/model quality.
participants.feedback: The DevOps side is clear, but I need evidence that viewer quality or scan success improves, not only deployment metrics.

participants.pattern: Evaluates deployability, health checks, and rollback readiness.
participants.feedback: The canary and health-check story is convincing. I would preserve Cloud Run logs as artifacts for final assessment.

participants.pattern: Looks for simple product-viewer value and avoids technical details.
participants.feedback: I understand the system is reliable, but I could not quickly see what a visitor would experience in the 360 viewer.
